claim: The per-proposal LAND verify step (tools/apply_proposal.py) shall, by default, run the T1 targeted-enforcer pytest subset resolved by tools/gate.py from the proposal's target enforced_by tuple (plus a small always-run determinism+smoke baseline), instead of the full pytest suite.
why: The full pytest suite paid a per-proposal LAND tax of roughly 100-300s dominated by Windows subprocess-spawn overhead (measured baseline: 614 tests / 298.76s wall; 63% of that mechanically eliminated by an unrelated in-process --help fix, not this requirement). Most proposals touch exactly one Requirement or Conflict whose enforced_by tuple already names the exact enforcer(s) that guard it -- the graph IS the test-impact map. Running only that targeted subset removes the redundant re-verification of unrelated tests on every single LAND without weakening what R-verify-closure-per-action actually checks for a targeted change, because R-land-gate-tier-selector-fails-closed guarantees the selector never narrows below what a full run would have exercised for THIS target. NOTE (found-issue, not fixed in this batch): the T1 selector logic IS ported -- internal/gate.SelectTier1 (internal/gate/gate.go) resolves the targeted enforcer subset from enforced_by and is tested by TestSelectTier1_MultipleChecksUnion. However the claim's 'by default in LAND' assertion is NOT realized in the Go port: hotam land (cmd/hotam/land.go) runs apply -> gen-spec -> all-violations only and does NOT call gate.SelectTier1; gate is a standalone manual command (hotam gate). The claim/why retain their Python-era wording pending a resolver decision on whether to downgrade enforcement or wire gate into land.
